name: create-skill-file
description: Guides Claude in creating well-structured SKILL.md files following best practices. Provides clear guidelines for naming, structure, and content organization to make skills easy to discover and execute.
capabilities: Provides templates for basic and workflow skills
Includes naming conventions and description guidelines
Shows examples of good vs bad skill design patterns
Contains quality checklists for skill validation
Documents error handling and troubleshooting approaches
author: labring
securitySummary: This skill contains only documentation and templates for creating skill files. It has no executable code, network calls, or file system access beyond its own directory. All 447 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES - the analyzer misidentified markdown code fences as Ruby backticks, documentation examples as cryptographic vulnerabilities, and documentation links as network threats. The content is purely educational.
valueStatement: Building AI skills is hard without proper structure. This guide provides proven templates and best practices for creating skills that work reliably and are easy to discover. It includes ready-to-use templates for basic and workflow skills.
limitations: Only provides documentation templates, no code execution
Focused on Claude skills, may not apply to other AI platforms
Templates require customization for specific use cases
Does not include automated skill testing capabilities

useCases.title: Create Custom AI Skills
useCases.description: Build specialized skills for your AI agents with proper documentation and structure

useCases.title: Standardize Skill Development
useCases.description: Establish consistent patterns for skill creation across your development team

useCases.title: Document AI Workflows
useCases.description: Create clear, actionable documentation for complex AI-assisted processes

promptTemplates.title: Basic Skill Creation
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating a simple file processing skill
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a skill that helps users process CSV files. Include when to use it, how it works, and examples of processing data.

promptTemplates.title: Workflow Skill Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a skill for deploying applications with validation, deployment, and rollback steps. Include decision points and error handling.

promptTemplates.title: Code Review Skill
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating a skill for reviewing code quality
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a skill that reviews Python code for security, performance, and maintainability. Include review criteria and examples.

promptTemplates.title: Project Conventions
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a skill that enforces project-specific coding conventions, imports, naming patterns, and error handling.

bestPractices: Keep skills focused on specific tasks with clear trigger keywords in description
Include concrete examples with real code, not abstract descriptions
Structure content with clear headings, lists, and code blocks
Test skills with actual scenarios before publishing
antiPatterns: Creating vague skills with generic names like helper or utility
Including basic programming knowledge Claude already knows
Writing tutorial-style content instead of actionable instructions
Forgetting to include verification steps and success criteria

faq.question: What makes a good skill name?
faq.answer: Use verb-noun format with specific terms. Include trigger keywords in description. Avoid generic words like helper.

faq.question: How long should a skill be?
faq.answer: Aim for 200-500 lines. Split into sub-files if longer. Keep main SKILL.md focused on essential information.

faq.question: Can I include executable scripts in my skill?
faq.answer: Yes, place scripts in a scripts directory and reference them from SKILL.md. Include usage instructions and parameter documentation.

faq.question: How do I test if my skill works?
faq.answer: Use keywords from your description in a conversation with Claude. Verify it activates and follows your instructions correctly.

faq.question: Should I include error handling in my skill?
faq.answer: Yes, always include troubleshooting section with common issues and solutions. Add validation steps to prevent errors.

faq.question: What is the difference between basic and workflow skills?
faq.answer: Basic skills handle simple tasks with linear steps. Workflow skills manage complex processes with decision points, validation, and error recovery.
